name: BytezTech Chat RAG Web Scrapt FAQ

parameters.content:
## Website FAQ Sync to Pinecone RAG Pipeline

### Required Credentials
* **OpenAI API:** Required for GPT-4o Q&A generation and text-embedding-3-small.
* **Pinecone API:** Requires a pre-created Pinecone index (1536 dimensions).

### Required Node Configurations
1.  **Get Sitemap Index:** Replace the placeholder URL with your actual XML sitemap URL.
2.  **Upsert FAQ Chunks to Pinecone:** Set your Pinecone index name and namespace.
3.  **OpenAI / Pinecone Nodes:** Select your connected credentials in all respective API nodes.
4.  *(Optional)* **Schedule Trigger:** Adjust the cron expression to change the sync frequency.
5.  *(Optional)* **Build GPT Request:** Edit the system prompt to match your desired output formatting.
6.  *(Optional)* **Flatten & Filter All URLs:** Modify the `skipList` variable to exclude specific website paths.

## Phase 1 – Sitemap Discovery

Fetches the XML sitemap index from your website, parses it into JSON, then extracts and iterates through each sub-sitemap URL to collect all individual page URLs.

**Configure:** Update the URL in ** Get Sitemap Index** to point to your own sitemap (e.g. `https://yoursite.com/sitemap_index.xml`).

## 🔗 Phase 2 – URL Filtering & Batch Loop

All collected page URLs are merged, deduplicated, and filtered to remove assets, admin paths, CDN files, and third-party links. Clean URLs are then passed into a batch loop (10 at a time) for efficient scraping.

➡️ **Tip:** Increase `batchSize` in ** Loop URLs in Batches** if your site has 100+ pages and your API rate limits allow it.

##  Phase 3 – Page Scraping & Content Extraction

Each URL is fetched as raw HTML. Script, style, nav, footer, and other non-content tags are stripped out. The node then extracts: page title, meta description, H1–H3 headings, body paragraphs, and list items — assembling clean text up to 5,000 characters.

Pages returning fewer than 100 characters of content are skipped automatically.

##  Phase 4 – AI FAQ Generation (GPT-4o)

The cleaned page text is sent to GPT-4o with a structured prompt that requires:
- At least one FAQ per major heading/section for full coverage
- Zero duplicate or overlapping questions
- Structured JSON output: `{ question, answer, topic, author }`

Each FAQ is assigned a deterministic `chunk_id` (based on URL + index) so that weekly re-runs safely overwrite existing vectors without creating duplicates in Pinecone.

 **Tip:** Edit the system prompt inside **  Build GPT Request ** to match your brand tone and company name.

##  Phase 5 – Embedding & Pinecone Upsert

Each validated FAQ chunk is embedded using **text-embedding-3-small** (1536 dimensions) and upserted into Pinecone using its deterministic `chunk_id` as the vector ID.

This ensures idempotent weekly syncs — re-running never creates duplicate vectors.

A **2-second wait** between each batch prevents OpenAI and Pinecone API rate-limit errors.

 **Configure:** Set your Pinecone **index name** and **namespace** inside ** Upsert FAQ Chunks to Pinecone**.

nodes.name: Schedule Trigger – Every Monday Midnight IST
nodes.notes: Cron: 30 18 * * 0 = Every Sunday 18:30 UTC = Monday 00:00 IST
